name: MAY-THURNER SYNDROME
definition: ILIAC VEIN COMPRESSION SYNDROME = ILEOCAVAL COMPRESSION SYNDROME = COCKETT obstruction of left common iliac vein caused by crossing of right common iliac artery

demographics.Path: spurlike projection in proximal left CIV in 22% (autopsy), in 49-62% of left
demographics.Incidence: 0.2% annually in patients evaluated for venous thrombosis of left lower extremity

dominant_finding: compression of left common iliac vein on CT / venography
discriminating_features: compression of left common iliac vein on CT / venography
tortuous pelvic venous collaterals joining contralateral veins

general: compression of left common iliac vein on CT / venography
tortuous pelvic venous collaterals joining contralateral veins

clinical.Prognosis: 60% of individuals with May-Thurner syndrome develop postthrombotic syndrome
clinical.Cx: deep vein thrombosis (L÷R = 5÷1), chronic venous stasis ulcer; pulmonary embolism;
